title: Blumea obliqua
taxonomy: Blumea obliqua is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It is a member of the genus Blumea, which consists of approximately 80 species of plants. This genus is further grouped into the tribe Inuleae and the subfamily Asteroideae. The classification of Blumea obliqua reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other plants in the Asteraceae family. The classification of Blumea obliqua is based on a combination of morphological and molecular studies.
uses: Blumea obliqua has been traditionally used in various parts of Asia for its medicinal properties. The plant's leaves and roots are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help in treating wounds and skin infections. intro: Blumea obliqua, also known as the Asian globe thistle, is a perennial plant native to Southeast Asia that is valued for its medicinal properties. The plant is rich in health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and protect against oxidative stress.
